creator: nakai@FreeBSD.org
text:
Now you can download
 
 ftp://mland98.rc.kyushu-u.ac.jp/pub/linux/NewLinux/SOURCES/
 
 but the author is now making sure the gogo source code distribution
 is really legal. If it is illegal, we never support this gogo ports.
 
 On 29 Nov 2000 06:04:09 -0000
 "Jason R Mastaler" <jason@mastaler.com> wrote:
 
 > 
 > >Submitter-Id:   current-users
 > >Originator:     Jason R Mastaler
 > >Organization:   
 > >Confidential:   no 
 > >Synopsis:       invalid download location for gogo source
 > >Severity:       serious
 > >Priority:       high
 > >Category:       ports
 > >Release:        FreeBSD 4.2-RELEASE i386
 > >Class:          sw-bug
 > >Environment: 
 > 
 > >Description: 
 > 
 > >> gogo236.tgz doesn't seem to exist in /usr/ports/distfiles/.
 > >> Attempting to fetch from http://homepage1.nifty.com/herumi/soft/gogo2/src/.
 > fetch: gogo236.tgz: Not Found
 > >> Attempting to fetch from ftp://ftp.FreeBSD.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/distfiles/.
 > fetch: gogo236.tgz: File unavailable (e.g., file not found, no access)
 > >> Couldn't fetch it - please try to retrieve this
 > >> port manually into /usr/ports/distfiles/ and try again.
 > *** Error code 1
 > 
 > Stop in /usr/ports/audio/gogo.
 > *** Error code 1
 > 
 > Stop in /usr/ports/audio/gogo.
 > *** Error code 1
 > 
 > Stop in /usr/ports/audio/gogo.
 > 
 > >How-To-Repeat: 
 > 
 > cd /usr/ports/audio/gogo && make fetch
 > 
 > >Fix: 
 > 
 > Update the source download location.
 > 
 > 
 
 
 ---
 Nakai
